Notice Of Motion To Enter Default; And Proof Of Service

Motion To Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution And To Return Property Levied Upon; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Mike Chappars, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF MIKE CHAPPARS (408)508-3447 mike@345law.com, Attorney for All Defendants
No other appearances

Hearing is held. Discussed, no ARGUED. The Court modifes the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRUE RENEWABLE ENERGY INCORPORATED A CALIPORNIA Motion To Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution And To Return Property Levied Upon

Defendant's "Motion To Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution And To Return Property Levied Upon" is granted. All other funds must be returned to defendant.

Judge Quinn is unavailable to hear this motion. Even if CCP 1008 applies, the court exercises its inherent power to grant the requested relief. (See Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094, 1107 [court's inherent power to reconsider orders on its own motion].)

The funds that plaintiff levied upon should have been released once the court vacated the default and default judgment. (Lapique v. Plummer (1920) 50 Cal.App. 88, 94 [once judgment is vacated it is not effective for any purpose]; Holmes v. Williams (1954) 127 Cal.App.2d 377, 379 ["A person who has conferred a benefit upon another in compliance with a judgment, or whose property has been taken thereunder, is entitled to restitution if the judgment is reversed or set aside"].)

The court denies plaintiff's request to deposit the funds with the court. Plaintiff is effectively seeking a writ of attachment without complying with the statutory mandates for that relief.

order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Or Vacate Default Judgment, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Via Zoom Conference
Paul Suilver, Esq. 510-701-0171, Specially appearing for Plaintiff,
No other appearances

Discussed. Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as modified:

DEFENDANT TRUE RENEWABLE ENERGY INCORPORATED A CALIPORNIA Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Or Vacate Default Judgment, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities.

Defendant's motion to vacate the default and default judgment entered on May 2, 2023, is granted.

The court rejects plaintiff's argument that the motion was not timely served. Plaintiff does not clearly articulate how the motion was untimely. In any event, plaintiff addresses the merits and waived any purported defect. (Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 ["It is well settled that the appearance of a party at the hearing of a motion and his or her opposition to the motion on its merits is a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion. (Citations.) This rule applies even when no notice was given at all."]

The court has a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. (See Fasuyi v. Permatex Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 694-696; Berman v. Klassman (1971) 17 Cal.App.3d 900, 909.) Defendant filed this motion within a "reasonable time" after entry of the default/default judgment. (CCP 473(b).) The Nahim declaration demonstrates mistake, inadvertence, and/or excusable neglect. Objection 2 to that declaration is sustained. The remaining objections are overruled. Defendant provides a proposed answer.

Pursuant to CCP 473(c)(1)(C), defendant shall pay $816.15 in fees and costs as reasonable expenses for obtaining the default/default judgment, payable within 30 days. (Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 822 ["Section 473 allows the court to impose just conditions in setting aside a default. Such conditions might include the attorney fees and costs incurred by the plaintiff in obtaining the default judgment.") The request for a penalty is denied.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)
